title: The Eighteen Hung King Generations
summary: After Lac Long Quan and Au Co separated, the eldest son ascended the throne, taking the title of Hung King and establishing the Van Lang state.

slides.text: Lac Long Quan and Au Co parted ways; 50 children followed their father to the sea, and 50 followed their mother to the mountains to govern the land together.
slides.imagePrompt: Lac Long Quan and Au Co saying goodbye, 50 children following father to the sea, 50 following mother to the mountains, mythical atmosphere, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The eldest son followed his mother to the mountains and was honored as king, with the title of Hung King, establishing the capital at Phong Chau (modern-day Phu Tho).
slides.imagePrompt: The eldest son being crowned as Hung Vuong king, ancient Vietnamese ceremony, Phong Chau capital background, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The first state of the Vietnamese people was named Van Lang, marking the beginning of our nation.
slides.imagePrompt: Ancient Van Lang kingdom map or landscape, primitive but peaceful village, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The Hung Kings and the people reclaimed wasteland, planted wet rice, and tamed wild beasts.
slides.imagePrompt: Ancient Vietnamese people farming, planting rice, and taming a wild buffalo, hard working and happy, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The Lac Viet people lived in villages, built dikes to prevent floods, and helped one another.
slides.imagePrompt: Lac Viet people building a dike together to stop flood water, unity and community spirit, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The Hung King era lasted through 18 generations of kings, each contributing to building a strong country.
slides.imagePrompt: A succession of Hung Kings standing proudly, symbolizing 18 generations, strong and wise leaders, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Since then, our people have had the custom of worshipping ancestors, always remembering our roots and the merit of the nation's founders.
slides.imagePrompt: Ancient Vietnamese family paying respect to their ancestors, traditional ceremony, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Every year, on the 10th day of the 3rd lunar month, the people across the country look toward the Hung Temple for the Ancestors' Anniversary.
slides.imagePrompt: Modern day people celebrating Hung Kings Festival, colorful parade, Hung Temple mountain background, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: Where did the Hung King establish the capital?
What was the name of our nation's first state?
How many Hung King generations were there in total?
When is the Hung Kings' Anniversary?
options: The 10th day of the 3rd Lunar month
The Full Moon of the 8th month
slides.answer: The 10th day of the 3rd Lunar month
